title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Client Server Roles
summary: A fleet-control CLI calls an inventory service through a cache and gateway, but operators cannot tell whether failures come from naming, transport, status, or decoding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go client server roles increment in a Go 1.26 net/http protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
